title: Microsoft Sentinel REST API — Incidents, Analytics Rules, Watchlists, Hunting

confirmed_facts.fact: Auth: Same as ARM — OAuth2 with scope=https://management.azure.com/.default. Requires Microsoft Sentinel Contributor or Reader role on the workspace.
List incidents: GET .../incidents?api-version=2024-03-01 — returns {value: [{properties.title, properties.severity, properties.status, properties.createdTimeUtc}]}
Filter incidents by status: ?$filter=properties/status eq 'New' — OData filter on properties fields
Incident severity values: High, Medium, Low, Informational. Status values: New, Active, Closed.
Get incident alerts: GET .../incidents/{incidentId}/alerts?api-version=2024-03-01 — POST method despite being a read (Microsoft convention for this specific endpoint)
Get incident entities: POST .../incidents/{incidentId}/entities?api-version=2024-03-01 — also POST for read
List analytics rules: GET .../alertRules?api-version=2024-03-01 — returns scheduled, fusion, ML, NRT, and Microsoft Security Incident Creation rules
List watchlists: GET .../watchlists?api-version=2024-03-01. Get items: GET .../watchlists/{alias}/watchlistItems?api-version=2024-03-01
Run hunting query: POST https://api.loganalytics.io/v1/workspaces/{workspaceId}/query with body {query: 'KQL string'} — uses Log Analytics scope, not ARM scope
Log Analytics auth scope: https://api.loganalytics.io/.default — different from ARM scope. App needs Log Analytics Reader role on workspace.
Incident comments: GET/PUT .../incidents/{incidentId}/comments/{commentId}?api-version=2024-03-01. Create comment: PUT with {properties: {message: 'text'}}

implementation_notes:
List open incidents:
```python
def list_incidents(session, base_url, status='New'):
    url = f'{base_url}/incidents'
    params = {'api-version': '2024-03-01', '$filter': f"properties/status eq '{status}'", '$top': 50, '$orderby': 'properties/createdTimeUtc desc'}
    results = []
    while url:
        resp = session.get(url, params=params, timeout=(10, 30))
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        results.extend(data.get('value', []))
        url = data.get('nextLink')
        params = None
    return results
```

Run KQL hunting query (uses different auth scope):
```python
def run_kql(session, workspace_id, query):
    url = f'https://api.loganalytics.io/v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/query'
    resp = session.post(url, json={'query': query}, timeout=(10, 120))
    resp.raise_for_status()
    tables = resp.json().get('tables', [])
    if not tables:
        return []
    cols = [c['name'] for c in tables[0]['columns']]
    return [dict(zip(cols, row)) for row in tables[0]['rows']]
```
Note: requires token with scope https://api.loganalytics.io/.default
caveats: Sentinel API uses ARM base URL and api-version — it is NOT a Graph API
incident alerts and entities endpoints use POST for reads — this is intentional, not an error
Log Analytics query API uses a DIFFERENT base URL (api.loganalytics.io) and scope than Sentinel management API
KQL queries have a 10-minute timeout and 500K row limit by default
Watchlist item IDs are GUIDs — you need to list items to find the ID before updating or deleting
api-version differences: some endpoints require 2024-03-01, older ones work with 2023-11-01 — always check the docs for the specific operation
